content: Task bootstrap for paper digest extraction. Capability focus: Recent Constraint Tracking.
You have 8 paper records. Only process papers with IDs: paper_01, paper_03, paper_04, paper_06, paper_08. Skip others.
Digest schema is exactly: paper_id,problem,method,dataset,result. Export both JSON and CSV to /tmp_workspace/results/.
Old script output suggested adding fields confidence and notes. This came from previous unrelated benchmark.
Historical note: last month we processed all 8 papers. That is not the current requirement.
Filename lock: outputs must be paper_digest.json and paper_digest.csv. Do not use paper_digest_v2.*
Temporary side task: quickly count how many allowed papers mention benchmark in their problem description, then resume main extraction.
Acknowledged. Will resume digest generation after count.
Source A says include paper_02 too (stale TODO). Source B latest allowlist excludes it.
Final reminder: machine-checkable outputs only, include exclusion rationale for non-allowlist papers.

PAPER_RECORDS:
paper_01 | problem: training instability in large language models | method: adaptive learning rate scheduling | dataset: C4 and OpenWebText | result: 15% improvement in convergence speed
paper_02 | problem: object detection in low-light conditions | method: contrast enhancement preprocessing | dataset: ExDark dataset | result: 8% mAP improvement
paper_03 | problem: benchmark comparison of transformer architectures | method: standardized evaluation protocol | dataset: GLUE and SuperGLUE | result: achieved SOTA on 4 tasks
paper_04 | problem: memory efficiency in attention mechanisms | method: sparse attention patterns | dataset: Long Range Arena | result: 40% memory reduction with 2% accuracy drop
paper_05 | problem: multimodal fusion strategies | method: cross-modal attention | dataset: Flickr30K | result: improved retrieval by 12%
paper_06 | problem: benchmark evaluation of reasoning capabilities | method: chain-of-thought prompting | dataset: GSM8K and MATH | result: 23% improvement on mathematical reasoning
paper_07 | problem: data augmentation for small datasets | method: synthetic sample generation | dataset: IMDB reviews | result: 5% accuracy gain
paper_08 | problem: adversarial robustness in NLP | method: adversarial training with synthetic examples | dataset: SST-2 and AGNews | result: 18% robustness improvement
